behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/reference-resolution/function-evaluator interaction over a cell range, not just isolated arithmetic. No real OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML output dependence. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is purely in-memory formula evaluation (`=SUM(A1:A10)`).
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook, set A1..A10 values, set target cell to `=SUM(A1:A10)`, run evaluator, assert computed value equals expected sum.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., `=B1+1` where B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff: reference resolver + dependency evaluation across multiple formula cells. Unit tests of a single arithmetic function would miss cascade bugs. Rejected higher levels because behavior is in-process formula propagation only.
behaviors.test_description: In one in-memory sheet, set `A1=3`, `B1==A1*2`, `C1==B1+1`; evaluate and assert `B1=6`, `C1=7`.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection correctness depends on dependency-graph construction plus evaluator interaction across cells. A single-function unit test is insufficient for the real failure mode. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow since no OS/network/model involvement is described.
behaviors.test_description: Set `A1==B1+1` and `B1==A1+1` in-memory, evaluate sheet, assert a circular-reference error is produced and evaluation terminates.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic function semantics (exact match / not found) and can be caught at built-in-function boundary without cross-module infra. Rejected Integration because behavior text is about VLOOKUP result rules, not dependency orchestration or state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Call VLOOKUP evaluator with a local table fixture for one present key and one absent key; assert matched value and not-found error.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., `=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")`).
behaviors.justification: Nested IF behavior is local deterministic expression/function evaluation. No required real infra, no model output dependence, no multi-stage orchestration. Rejected Integration because no module-boundary-only failure is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate nested IF expressions with representative A1 values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and assert outputs `low`, `medium`, `high`.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: This is local string-composition policy for one built-in function. Real failure is in function logic; no OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require cross-module orchestration to manifest.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate CONCATENATE with inputs containing normal and empty-cell values plus separator; assert output omits empty entries and uses correct separator placement.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., `=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is cross-module workbook/sheet reference resolution plus evaluator handoff. Unit-only math parsing would miss sheet-lookup wiring failures. Rejected higher levels since behavior is in-memory workbook linkage with no real infra or AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with Sheet1 and Sheet2, set `Sheet2!A1=10`, set `Sheet1!B1==Sheet2!A1+1`, evaluate and assert `Sheet1!B1=11`.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on dependency graph invalidation/recompute ordering across modules (cell store, graph, evaluator). A local single-function test cannot expose ordering propagation bugs. Rejected System because no real OS/process/network requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build dependency chain/diamond in-memory, evaluate once, change a source cell, trigger recompute, assert updated dependent values and that stale intermediate values are not observed.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at interaction boundary between undo/redo history and recalculation engine/state restoration. Unit-testing history stack alone would miss dependent-value restoration regressions. Rejected higher levels: behavior is local engine state management, no OS/network/model.
behaviors.test_description: Set dependent formulas, evaluate, edit an upstream formula, re-evaluate, perform undo, then assert restored formula text and restored dependent computed values across affected cells.

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real disk persistence (`saving ... to disk and loading it back`) and binary serialization fidelity, including float precision. Mocked/in-memory fakes can hide filesystem and real I/O serialization issues. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML decision steps are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Write a workbook with formulas and high-precision floating-point values to a real temp file using the binary format, reload it through normal load path, and assert exact formula text/value equivalence plus precision tolerance policy.
